login.errorLocked: Account is locked. Try again later.
login.errorRateLimited: Too many login attempts. Please wait a few minutes.

animalForm.fromCalvingBanner: Registering offspring from birth event. All fields can be changed.

financial.title: Financial

fertility.title: Fertility
fertility.calvingIntervalDesc: Days between successive calvings per cow. Target is under 400 days.
fertility.daysOpenDesc: Days from calving until the cow is confirmed pregnant again.
fertility.reproStatusDesc: Current pregnancy and breeding status across the herd.
fertility.conceptionTrendDesc: Percentage of cows that conceive on the first service, by month.
fertility.expectedCalvingsDesc: Upcoming calvings based on confirmed pregnancy dates.

health.title: Herd Health
health.resolutionByTypeDesc: Average days to resolve each issue type
health.cureRateTrendDesc: Monthly % of issues that get resolved
health.incidenceTrendDesc: Cases per 100 cows/month for top diseases
health.treatmentCostPerCowDesc: Monthly spend normalized by herd size
health.slowestToResolveDesc: Cows with the longest avg resolution time

structure.title: Herd Structure
structure.mortalityRateDesc: Sold + dead as % of herd per month

medications.title: Medications
medications.name: Medication Name
medications.deactivateConfirm: Deactivate {name}? It will no longer appear in the treatment form.
medications.deleteConfirm: Permanently delete {name}? This cannot be undone. Deletion is blocked if the medication has treatment records.

treatments.cowPlaceholder: Search for cow by tag or name...
treatments.noWithdrawal: No withdrawal period for selected medications

withdrawal.milkBanner: {count} cow(s) on milk withdrawal — DO NOT USE MILK
withdrawal.meatBanner: {count} animal(s) on meat withdrawal — DO NOT SLAUGHTER
withdrawal.allClearSub: No animals are currently on any withdrawal

common.errorLoading: Failed to load. Please try again.
common.noPermission: You don't have permission for this action

sync.staleWarning: Data may be outdated — connect to sync
sync.queueOverflow: Many changes pending — connect to sync soon
sync.offlineBanner: You are working offline. Changes will sync when connected.
sync.cacheReset: Local cache was reset. Your data will re-sync from the server.
sync.cannotLogin: Cannot log in — no connection available

healthIssues.title: Health Issues
healthIssues.cowPlaceholder: Search for cow by tag or name...
healthIssues.deleteConfirm: Permanently delete this health issue record?

issueTypes.name: Name
issueTypes.deleteConfirm: Delete issue type "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
issueTypes.deactivateConfirm: Deactivate "{name}"? It will no longer appear when logging issues.

milkRecording.title: Milk Recording
milkRecording.sessionTimeHint: All entries will be logged at this time

breeding.dismissConfirm: Dismiss this alert? It will not appear again.
breeding.dismissAllConfirm: Dismiss all {count} overdue alerts? They will not appear again.

form.expectedCalvingHint: Pre-filled from last insemination. You can override.

settings.title: Settings
settings.issueTypesDesc: Configure health issue types and categories
settings.breedTypesDesc: Manage breed types and their timing defaults
settings.auditLogDesc: View change history for users, cows, and settings
settings.reportsDesc: Generate PDF and Excel reports for audits and analysis
settings.milkPriceDesc: Used for revenue calculations in analytics
settings.exportDataDesc: Download a JSON backup of all farm data
settings.forceSyncDesc: Re-download all data from the server

errors.network: No connection — please check your network
errors.permissionDenied: You don't have permission to do this

breeding.name: Breeding
breeding.desc: Breeding hub, events, and reproduction tracking

milkRecording.name: Milk Recording
milkRecording.desc: Daily milk recording and yield tracking

breedTypes.title: Breed Types
breedTypes.name: Name
breedTypes.deleteConfirm: Delete this breed type? This cannot be undone.
breedTypes.deleteBlocked: Cannot delete: this breed is assigned to {count} cow(s).

users.title: User Management
users.confirmDeactivate: Deactivate {name}? They will no longer be able to log in.
users.confirmReactivate: Reactivate {name}? They will be able to log in again.
users.confirmDelete: Permanently delete {name}? This cannot be undone.
users.selfDeactivateBlocked: You cannot deactivate your own account
users.selfRoleBlocked: You cannot change your own role
users.revokeSessionsConfirm: This will log out {name} from all devices immediately.

profile.title: Profile
profile.logoutConfirm: Are you sure you want to log out?

twoFactor.title: Two-Factor Authentication
twoFactor.subtitle: Enter the code from your authenticator app
twoFactor.setupSubtitle: Secure your super-admin account with two-factor authentication
twoFactor.scanQR: Scan this QR code with your authenticator app
twoFactor.manualEntry: Can't scan? Enter this code manually
twoFactor.confirmCode: Enter the 6-digit code from your app
twoFactor.saveCodesWarning: Save these recovery codes in a secure place. Each code can only be used once.

treatmentHistory.name: Treatment History
treatmentHistory.desc: Complete traceability of all treatments, medications, costs and vet visits

discardedMilk.name: Discarded Milk
discardedMilk.desc: Accountability log of all discarded milk with reasons

medicationUsage.name: Medication Usage
medicationUsage.desc: Usage frequency and cost breakdown per medication

milkProduction.name: Milk Production
milkProduction.desc: Per-cow production summary with daily and session averages

breeding.name: Breeding & Reproduction
breeding.desc: All breeding events including inseminations, preg checks and calvings

herdHealth.name: Herd Health Summary
herdHealth.desc: Health issues, severity, resolution times and linked treatments

superAdmin.farmCodeHint: 3-10 uppercase alphanumeric characters. Used for login.
superAdmin.revokeAllConfirm: This will immediately log out all users of this farm. Are you sure?
superAdmin.errorTimeout: Request timed out — please try again
superAdmin.deactivateConfirm: Deactivating this farm will prevent all users from logging in. Are you sure?
superAdmin.deleteConfirmMessage: This will PERMANENTLY delete farm "{name}" and ALL its data (cows, milk records, treatments, health issues, breeding events, users, etc). This cannot be undone.

steps.heatDetection: Cow shows heat signs — log it
steps.notPregnant: Not pregnant — back to heat watch

negativePreg.title: Negative Pregnancy Check
negativePreg.desc: If the pregnancy check is negative, the cow goes back to heat watch. She will need to be re-inseminated at her next heat cycle.

abortion.title: Abortion
abortion.desc: If a cow aborts, log the event and get a vet check. After a recovery period, she returns to the breeding cycle.

repeatBreeder.title: Repeat Breeder
repeatBreeder.desc: A cow that has been inseminated 3 or more times without conceiving is called a repeat breeder. Consult your vet — there may be an underlying fertility issue.

missedHeat.title: Missed Heat
missedHeat.desc: If you miss a cow's heat, she will cycle again in about 21 days. Watch for the next heat signs. The app will alert you when the next heat is expected.

recording-milk.title: Recording Milk
recording-milk.what: Record how many litres each cow produces at each milking session.
recording-milk.when: Every time you milk your cows — typically twice daily (morning and afternoon sessions).

flow.showWarning: Warning shown — milk may need discarding

steps: Tap 'Milk' in the bottom navigation to open the milk recording page.
Select the session — AM for morning milking, PM for afternoon. The app remembers today's date automatically.
Search for a cow by tag number or name using the search dropdown.
Enter the number of litres the cow produced. If the milk must be discarded (e.g. withdrawal), tick the discard box.
Tap 'Save'. If the cow is on medication withdrawal, the app warns you so you know the milk may not be safe to sell.
Repeat for each cow. You can see a summary of the session totals at the top of the page.
recording-milk.next: The record is saved to the server (or queued offline). You can view it later in Milk History. The dashboard shows today's total litres.
tips: You can only record one entry per cow per session per day — duplicates are blocked.
If you're offline, records save locally and sync when you're back online.
The withdrawal warning comes from treatments — if a cow was treated with medication, the system knows the withdrawal period.
You can edit a record you made, but only admins can delete records.

milk-history.title: Viewing Milk History
milk-history.what: Browse and search past milk records with filters and sorting.
milk-history.when: When you want to check production trends, find specific records, or review daily totals.

flow.sortResults: Sort by date, litres, or tag number

steps: From the Milk page, tap 'History' to open the milk history view.
Use the filter chips to narrow results — filter by date range, session (AM/PM), specific cow, or who recorded it.
Browse records in the list. Each card shows the cow, litres, session, date, and who recorded it.
Tap column headers to sort by recording date, litres, or tag number.
Use the search bar to find records by cow tag number, cow name, or recorder name.
milk-history.next: The history view is read-only for workers. Admins can delete records from here. For detailed production reports, use the Reports section in Settings.
tips: Use the 'Discarded' filter to see only milk that was marked as discarded (e.g. due to withdrawal).
The pagination shows 20 records per page — use the page controls at the bottom to navigate.
For detailed analytics on milk production trends, visit the Analytics section.

logging-health-issue.title: Logging a Health Issue
logging-health-issue.what: Record when a cow is sick or showing health problems.
logging-health-issue.when: When you notice a cow is unwell — limping, low appetite, swollen udder, fever, or any other symptom.

flow.selectType: Choose the issue type (e.g. Mastitis, Lameness)
flow.save: Issue logged — appears in Open Issues

steps: Navigate to 'Log Health Issue' from the dashboard or cow detail page.
Search and select the cow that is sick.
Choose the type of issue from the dropdown (e.g. Mastitis, Lameness, Respiratory). These are customizable by your admin.
Set the severity level — this helps prioritize which cows need attention first.
If it's a mastitis or teat-related issue, use the teat selector to mark which teats are affected (front-left, front-right, etc.).
Add detailed notes about what you observed — this helps the vet or manager understand the situation.
Tap Save. The issue now appears in the Open Issues list.
logging-health-issue.next: The health issue is tracked as 'open' until resolved. You can add treatments linked to this issue. The cow appears in health analytics and may trigger reports.
tips: Be as specific as possible in your notes — describe symptoms, temperature, behavior changes.
You can log multiple issues for the same cow if she has different problems.
The issue type determines how it's tracked in analytics — your admin can customize the available types.
Severity helps prioritize: Critical issues should get immediate attention.

adding-treatment.title: Adding a Treatment
adding-treatment.what: Record medication or treatment given to a cow.
adding-treatment.when: When you administer medication, call the vet, or apply any treatment to a sick cow.
steps: Navigate to 'Log Treatment' from the dashboard, cow detail, or health issue detail page.
Select the cow receiving treatment.
Choose the medication from the dropdown. Your admin manages the medication list.
Enter the dosage given and the cost of the treatment.
Optionally mark if a vet visited and link to an existing health issue.
Tap Save. The system automatically calculates when the milk and meat withdrawal periods end.
adding-treatment.next: The withdrawal dates are calculated from the medication's settings. During the withdrawal period, the milk recording page will warn you that this cow's milk may not be safe to sell.
tips: Always select the correct medication — the withdrawal days are different for each one.
Linking a treatment to a health issue helps track which treatments resolved which problems.
The cost field helps with financial analytics — you can see treatment spending trends.
You can add extra medications to a treatment if multiple drugs were used.

withdrawal-periods.title: Understanding Withdrawal Periods
withdrawal-periods.what: After treatment, milk and meat have a safety period before they can be sold. The app tracks this automatically.
withdrawal-periods.when: You don't need to do anything — the system handles this. But understand it so you know why warnings appear.

flow.milkSafe: Milk is safe — no more warnings

steps: When you record a treatment, the system looks at the medication's withdrawal days (set by your admin).
It calculates the date when milk withdrawal ends and when meat withdrawal ends.
During the withdrawal period, the milk recording page shows a warning badge on the cow.
If you record milk for a cow on withdrawal, the app suggests marking it as discarded.
Once the withdrawal period passes, the warning disappears automatically.
withdrawal-periods.next: Withdrawal information shows in the Withdrawal Alerts page and in milk recording. Discarded milk appears in the Discarded Milk report.
tips: Different medications have different withdrawal periods — some are just a few days, others are weeks.
The withdrawal page shows all cows currently on withdrawal in one place.
Milk withdrawal and meat withdrawal can have different durations for the same medication.
If in doubt about selling milk or meat, always consult your vet.

resolving-health-issue.title: Resolving a Health Issue
resolving-health-issue.what: Mark a health issue as resolved when the cow has recovered.
resolving-health-issue.when: When a sick cow has recovered and no longer shows symptoms.

flow.selectIssue: Tap on the issue to view details

steps: Go to the Open Issues page from the dashboard.
Find and tap the issue you want to resolve.
On the issue detail page, change the status from 'Open' to 'Resolved'.
The system records the resolution date and calculates how long it took to resolve.
resolving-health-issue.next: The issue moves from the open list to the resolved list. Resolution time is tracked in health analytics — faster resolution means better herd health management.
tips: Only resolve an issue when the cow has truly recovered — premature resolution skews your analytics.
If the same issue comes back within 60 days, the system counts it as a recurrence.
Resolution time data helps identify which issue types take longest to treat.

breeding-lifecycle.title: Breeding Lifecycle Overview
breeding-lifecycle.what: The complete breeding cycle for a dairy cow — from heat detection through calving and back again.
breeding-lifecycle.when: Read this to understand the full picture of breeding management before using the individual breeding features.
breeding-lifecycle.next: Each step in this cycle has its own help page with detailed instructions. Tap any breeding topic in the Help Library to learn more.
tips: The typical cycle is: Heat → Insemination → Pregnancy Check → Dry-Off → Calving → Repeat.
The app calculates dates automatically based on your breed type settings — different breeds have different gestation periods.
Notifications alert you when the next step is due — check the Breeding Notifications page regularly.
A cow that doesn't conceive after 3+ inseminations may need veterinary attention.
The system tracks all these events and provides fertility analytics to help you improve conception rates.

logging-heat.title: Logging Heat Detection
logging-heat.what: Record when a cow shows signs of being in heat (estrus).
logging-heat.when: When you observe heat signs — mounting, restlessness, mucus discharge, reduced milk, standing to be mounted.

flow.goToBreeding: Go to Breeding Hub → Log Event
flow.systemCalc: System calculates next heat and insemination window

steps: Observe your cows for heat signs — common signs include mounting other cows, restlessness, mucus discharge, and standing heat.
Open the Breeding Hub and tap 'Log Event'.
Search and select the cow showing heat.
Choose 'Heat Detection' as the event type.
Add any notes about the signs you observed.
Save. The system calculates when the next heat is expected (typically 21 days) and suggests the optimal insemination window.
logging-heat.next: The system creates reminders for insemination timing. If you plan to inseminate, do it within 12-18 hours of heat onset for best results.
tips: Heat detection is best done early morning and late evening when cows are less active.
A cow's heat cycle is approximately 21 days — the app tracks this pattern.
Standing heat (when a cow stands still while being mounted) is the most reliable sign.
Log heat even if you don't plan to inseminate — it helps track the cow's cycle regularity.

logging-insemination.title: Logging Insemination
logging-insemination.what: Record when a cow is artificially inseminated (AI) or served by a bull.
logging-insemination.when: After insemination has been performed — either by an AI technician or natural bull service.

flow.startLog: Go to Breeding Hub → Log Event
flow.enterSireAI: Enter AI sire details (straw number, sire name)
flow.systemCalc: System calculates preg check and calving dates

steps: Open the Breeding Hub and tap 'Log Event'.
Select the cow that was inseminated.
Choose 'AI Insemination' or 'Bull Service' as the event type.
For AI: Enter the sire name and straw/batch number. For bull: Enter the bull name or ID.
Add any relevant notes.
Save. The system automatically calculates the expected pregnancy check date (~30 days) and potential calving date.
logging-insemination.next: A pregnancy check reminder will be created for approximately 30 days later. The expected calving date is calculated based on the breed's gestation period.
tips: Log the insemination as soon as possible after it happens for accurate date calculations.
The pregnancy check date and calving date are calculated from the breed type's settings — different breeds have different gestation periods.
Keep sire records accurate — this is important for breeding management and avoiding inbreeding.
If using AI, record the straw number for traceability.

pregnancy-check.title: Pregnancy Check Results
pregnancy-check.what: Record the result of a pregnancy check — positive (pregnant) or negative (not pregnant).
pregnancy-check.when: After your vet or technician has performed a pregnancy check on a cow (typically 28-35 days after insemination).

flow.startLog: Go to Breeding Hub → Log Event

steps: Open the Breeding Hub and tap 'Log Event'.
Select the cow that was checked.
Choose 'Pregnancy Check Positive' or 'Pregnancy Check Negative'.
For positive results: confirm or adjust the expected calving date. The app pre-calculates it from the insemination date.
Save the result.
pregnancy-check.next: If positive: the system calculates the dry-off date (typically 60 days before calving) and creates reminders. If negative: the cow returns to the breeding cycle for re-insemination.
tips: A positive pregnancy check is a key milestone — it triggers automatic dry-off and calving reminders.
If the cow is not pregnant, she should be watched for the next heat cycle.
You can adjust the expected calving date if the vet gives a different estimate.
The expected calving date is used to calculate the dry-off date automatically.

dry-off.title: Dry-Off
dry-off.what: Record when a pregnant cow is dried off (stops being milked) before calving.
dry-off.when: Typically 45-60 days before the expected calving date. The app will send you a notification.

flow.notification: System alerts: time to dry off
flow.goToBreeding: Go to Breeding Hub → Log Event
flow.statusChange: Cow status changes — no longer milked

steps: Check your Breeding Notifications for cows approaching their dry-off date.
Open the Breeding Hub and tap 'Log Event'.
Select the cow to dry off.
Choose 'Dry Off' as the event type.
Save. The cow's status changes and she won't appear in the milking list.
dry-off.next: The cow enters the dry period before calving. The system continues tracking the expected calving date and will alert you when calving is approaching.
tips: Drying off too late can reduce the cow's next lactation performance.
Drying off too early means lost milk production.
The standard dry period is 45-60 days — your breed type settings determine the exact calculation.
During the dry period, monitor the cow for health issues but don't include her in milking.

logging-calving.title: Logging a Calving
logging-calving.what: Record when a cow gives birth to a calf.
logging-calving.when: When a cow calves — the app will have a notification near the expected calving date.

flow.goToBreeding: Go to Breeding Hub → Log Event
flow.cycleReset: Breeding cycle resets — cow ready for new cycle

steps: When a cow calves, go to the Breeding Hub and tap 'Log Event'.
Select the cow that gave birth.
Choose 'Calving' as the event type.
Enter details about the calf — sex and whether it was born alive.
Add any notes about the calving (e.g. difficulty, assistance needed).
Save. The cow's breeding cycle resets and she becomes active for milking again.
logging-calving.next: The cow enters a recovery period. After recovery (typically 45-60 days), watch for the next heat cycle to begin the breeding cycle again.
tips: Log calvings as soon as possible for accurate records.
The calving date resets the breeding timeline — the next heat is expected after the voluntary waiting period.
If the calf is a heifer, you may want to add her to the herd later as a replacement.
Difficult calvings should be noted — they may affect the cow's future breeding decisions.

breeding-notifications.title: Understanding Breeding Notifications
breeding-notifications.what: The app creates automatic alerts when breeding actions are due for your cows.
breeding-notifications.when: Check notifications regularly — at least daily during breeding season.

flow.systemAlerts: System generates alerts from breeding events

steps: Open the Breeding Hub and tap 'Notifications'.
Review the alerts — they are grouped by type: heats due, pregnancy checks due, calvings approaching, and dry-offs needed.
Overdue actions are highlighted — these need immediate attention.
For each alert, either tap to log the event or dismiss if not applicable.
Dismissed alerts won't show again for that specific cow/event combination.
breeding-notifications.next: Acting on a notification logs the breeding event and updates the cow's timeline. The system then calculates the next step in the cycle.
tips: Check notifications daily — overdue actions mean missed opportunities.
The 'Needs Attention' section shows cows that have overdue breeding actions.
A cow can appear in multiple notification categories at once.
Dismissing is useful when a cow was sold, or when you've decided not to breed her.

adding-cow.title: Adding a New Cow
adding-cow.what: Register a new cow in the system with her details.
adding-cow.when: When a new cow joins your herd — purchased, born on-farm, or transferred from another farm.

flow.fillForm: Fill in cow details (tag, name, sex, DOB)

steps: Go to the Cows page and tap the + (add) button.
Enter the tag number — this must be unique and is how you'll identify the cow.
Enter the cow's name (optional but helpful).
Select the sex — female for cows/heifers, male for bulls.
Enter the date of birth. If unknown, estimate as closely as possible.
Select the breed type from the dropdown.
Optionally enter sire and dam information for breeding records.
Tap Save.
adding-cow.next: The cow appears in your herd list and is available for milk recording, health tracking, and breeding management.
tips: Tag numbers must be unique — the system will reject duplicates.
The breed type affects breeding calculations (gestation period, heat cycles).
Setting the correct date of birth is important for age-based analytics.
You can edit cow details later from the cow's detail page.

cow-status.title: Understanding Cow Status
cow-status.what: Each cow has a status that tracks her current state in the herd.
cow-status.when: Understanding statuses helps you manage your herd and interpret the data correctly.

flow.active: Active — cow is part of the milking herd
flow.dryDesc: Cow is in dry period before calving
flow.removedDesc: Cow is removed from active herd

steps: Active: The cow is healthy and part of the milking herd. She can be milked and bred.
Dry: The cow is pregnant and has been dried off before calving. She won't appear in the milking list.
Sold: The cow has been sold and left the farm. She remains in records for history but is not active.
Dead: The cow has died. She remains in records for history and analytics.
Heifer: A young female cow that has not yet calved. Once she calves, she becomes 'Active'.
cow-status.next: Status changes affect which cows appear in milking, breeding, and analytics. The herd summary on the dashboard reflects current status counts.
tips: Status is usually updated automatically by breeding events (e.g. dry-off changes status to 'Dry').
You can also manually change a cow's status from her edit page.
Sold and dead cows are soft-deleted — they still appear in historical reports but not in active lists.
Only admins can mark a cow as sold or dead.

cow-details.title: Viewing Cow Details
cow-details.what: See all information about a specific cow — health history, breeding timeline, milk production, and more.
cow-details.when: When you need to check a cow's history, make decisions about treatment, or review her breeding status.

flow.viewTabs: Browse sections: Health, Breeding, Milk, Treatments

steps: Go to the Cows page and find the cow (use search or scroll).
Tap on the cow card to open her detail page.
The detail page shows key info at the top: tag number, name, breed, age, status.
Scroll down to see action buttons for logging health issues, treatments, and breeding events.
Tap on health, breeding, or treatment history links to see full timelines.
cow-details.next: From the detail page, you can navigate to any related feature — log a health issue, add a treatment, record breeding, or view milk history for that cow.
tips: The cow detail page is the central hub for everything about one cow.
If the cow is on milk withdrawal, a badge will show on her detail page.
Admins can edit cow details or soft-delete the cow from this page.
The reproductive timeline shows all breeding events in chronological order.

managing-users.title: Managing Users & Workers
managing-users.what: Add, edit, and manage user accounts for your farm team.
managing-users.when: When you need to add a new worker, change someone's permissions, or deactivate a user who left.
steps: Go to Settings → User Management.
To add a user, tap 'Add User'.
Choose the role: Admin (full access, password login) or Worker (limited access, PIN login).
For admins: set a username and password. For workers: set their name and a 4-digit PIN.
Assign permissions — workers can have specific permissions like 'Can Record Milk', 'Can Log Issues', etc.
Tap Save to create the user.
managing-users.next: The new user can now log in with their credentials. Workers use the PIN tab on the login screen. Admins use the password tab.
tips: Workers can only access features they have permission for — the rest is hidden.
You can deactivate users instead of deleting them — this preserves their historical records.
PINs must be exactly 4 digits.
You cannot change your own role or deactivate yourself.
Use 'Revoke Sessions' to force a user to log in again if their device was lost.

managing-breed-types.title: Managing Breed Types
managing-breed-types.what: Add and configure cattle breed types with their breeding timing parameters.
managing-breed-types.when: When you have a new breed on your farm, or need to adjust breeding calculation parameters.

flow.addEdit: Add or edit a breed type
flow.setTimings: Set gestation, heat cycle, preg check, dry-off days

steps: Go to Settings → Breed Types.
To add a new breed, tap 'Add'. To edit, tap the existing breed.
Enter the breed name (e.g. 'Holstein', 'Jersey').
Set the breeding parameters: gestation days, heat cycle length, pregnancy check days, and dry-off days.
These timing parameters control when the system calculates breeding reminders and expected dates.
Tap Save.
managing-breed-types.next: Cows assigned to this breed type will use these parameters for breeding date calculations. Existing cows are not retroactively updated.
tips: The code is auto-generated from the breed name and cannot be changed.
You cannot delete a breed type if any cows are assigned to it.
Default values: gestation ~283 days, heat cycle ~21 days, preg check ~30 days, dry-off ~60 days before calving.
These timings vary by breed — consult your vet for accurate values.

managing-issue-types.title: Managing Issue Types
managing-issue-types.what: Customize the health issue categories available when logging health problems.
managing-issue-types.when: When you need to add a new type of health issue that isn't in the system, or edit an existing one.

flow.addEdit: Add or edit an issue type

steps: Go to Settings → Issue Types.
To add, tap 'Add'. To edit, tap the existing type.
Enter the name (e.g. 'Bloat', 'Eye Infection') and choose an emoji icon.
Tap Save. The new type will appear in the health issue type dropdown.
managing-issue-types.next: The new issue type is immediately available when logging health issues. It will also appear in health analytics.
tips: The code is auto-generated from the name and cannot be changed after creation.
You cannot delete an issue type if health issues reference it — deactivate instead.
Choose a descriptive emoji that helps workers quickly identify the issue type.
Common types include: Mastitis, Lameness, Respiratory, Metabolic, Reproductive.

managing-medications.title: Managing Medications
managing-medications.what: Add and configure medications that can be used in treatments, including their withdrawal periods.
managing-medications.when: When you have a new medication on the farm, or need to update withdrawal days or costs.

flow.setWithdrawal: Set milk and meat withdrawal days

steps: Go to Settings → Medications.
To add, tap 'Add'. To edit, tap the existing medication.
Enter the medication name.
Set the milk withdrawal days — how many days after treatment before milk is safe to sell.
Set the meat withdrawal days — how many days before meat is safe.
Optionally set a default cost per treatment.
Tap Save.
managing-medications.next: The medication appears in the treatment form. When a treatment uses this medication, the system automatically calculates withdrawal end dates.
tips: Withdrawal days are critical for food safety — always use the values from the medication label or your vet's advice.
If a medication's withdrawal days change, update it here — future treatments will use the new values.
Deactivate medications you no longer use instead of deleting them — this preserves treatment history.
Cost data feeds into financial analytics for treatment spending reports.

feature-flags.title: Feature Flags
feature-flags.what: Enable or disable entire feature modules in the app.
feature-flags.when: When you want to simplify the app by hiding features you don't use, or enable features you're ready to start using.
steps: Go to Settings → Feature Flags.
You'll see toggles for each feature module: Breeding, Milk Recording, Health Issues, Treatments, Analytics.
Toggle a feature off to hide it from the app — the navigation items and routes are removed.
Toggle it back on to restore it. No data is lost when toggling.
Changes take effect immediately for all users on the farm.
feature-flags.next: Disabled features are hidden from navigation and inaccessible via URL. All data is preserved — re-enabling the feature brings everything back.
tips: Feature flags are useful for gradually rolling out the app — start with just Milk Recording, then add Health Issues, etc.
Disabling a feature doesn't delete any data.
Workers may need to refresh their app to see the changes.
If a worker tries to access a disabled feature via URL, they'll be redirected to the dashboard.

farm-settings.title: Farm Settings
farm-settings.what: Configure basic farm settings like the farm name and default language.
farm-settings.when: When you first set up the farm, or when you need to change the farm name or default language.

flow.editFields: Edit farm name or default language

steps: Go to Settings → Farm Settings.
Edit the farm name — this appears in the app header.
Set the default language — English or Afrikaans. This is the default for new users.
Tap Save.
farm-settings.next: The farm name updates throughout the app. The default language affects new user accounts — existing users keep their chosen language.
tips: Individual users can still choose their own language from their profile.
The farm name appears in reports and exports.

running-reports.title: Running Reports
running-reports.what: Generate PDF or Excel reports for various aspects of your farm data.
running-reports.when: For record-keeping, compliance, vet visits, or management review.
steps: Go to Settings → Reports.
Choose the report type: Treatment History, Discarded Milk, Medication Usage, Milk Production, Breeding, or Herd Health.
Set the date range — all reports require a 'from' and 'to' date.
Choose the format — PDF for printing, Excel for further analysis.
Tap 'Generate'. The report downloads to your device.
running-reports.next: The report file is downloaded and can be shared, printed, or filed for compliance records.
tips: Treatment History reports are useful for vet visits — they show all medications used.
Milk Production reports show per-cow production summaries.
Excel format allows you to do your own analysis in a spreadsheet.

audit-log.title: Audit Log
audit-log.what: View a record of all changes made in the system — who did what and when.
audit-log.when: When you need to investigate changes, verify who made a record, or for compliance auditing.

flow.setFilters: Filter by entity type, user, action, or date
flow.viewChanges: View change details (old vs new values)

steps: Go to Settings → Audit Log.
Use the filters to narrow results: entity type (cows, treatments, etc.), specific user, action type (create, update, delete), or date range.
Browse the log entries. Each entry shows who made the change, what was changed, and when.
Tap an entry to see the details — including old and new values for updates.
audit-log.next: The audit log is read-only — it's a permanent record of all changes. It cannot be edited or deleted.
tips: The audit log tracks all create, update, and delete actions across the system.
Use date range filters to narrow down to a specific time period.
The 'entity type' filter is helpful when investigating changes to a specific area (e.g. all cow record changes).
The audit log helps with accountability — you can always see who made a change.

welcome.title: Welcome to MyHerder!
welcome.desc: This is your dashboard — a quick overview of your herd. Let's walk through the key areas.

stats.title: Herd Summary
stats.desc: These chips show your active, dry, pregnant, and sick cow counts at a glance.

quickActions.title: Quick Actions
quickActions.desc: Tap any card to jump straight to that feature — view cows, record milk, log breeding events, and more.

nav.title: Navigation
nav.desc: Use the bottom bar to move between Home, Cows, Milk, and Breeding sections.

search.title: Search Cows
search.desc: Type a tag number or name to quickly find a specific animal.

filters.title: Filter by Status
filters.desc: Tap a chip to filter cows by status — active, sold, dead, or all.

cowCard.title: Cow Cards
cowCard.desc: Each card shows the cow's tag, name, and status. Tap a card to see full details.

addBtn.title: Add a Cow
addBtn.desc: Tap the + button to register a new animal in your herd.

session.title: Choose a Session
session.desc: Select AM or PM to record milk for the morning or afternoon session.

search.title: Find a Cow
search.desc: Use the search bar to quickly find a cow by tag number or name.

entryCard.title: Record Litres
entryCard.desc: Enter the litres for each cow. Cards turn green once saved.

history.title: View History
history.desc: Tap 'View History' to see past milk records, filter by date, and review trends.

filters.title: Filter Records
filters.desc: Use the date range and session filters to narrow down records.

search.title: Search Records
search.desc: Search by cow tag, name, or the person who recorded the milk.

records.title: Milk Records
records.desc: Each card shows the cow, litres, session, and who recorded it. Discarded records are flagged.

pagination.title: Browse Pages
pagination.desc: Use the pagination bar to navigate through older records.

cowSelect.title: Select a Cow
cowSelect.desc: Search and select the cow with the health issue.

issueType.title: Issue Type
issueType.desc: Choose the type of health issue — mastitis, lameness, respiratory, etc.

severity.title: Severity Level
severity.desc: Set the severity to help prioritize treatment: low, medium, or high.

teats.title: Affected Teats
teats.desc: For udder-related issues, tap the affected teats on the diagram.

save.title: Save the Issue
save.desc: Add any notes and tap Save. The issue will appear in the cow's health history.

cowSelect.title: Select a Cow
cowSelect.desc: Search and select the cow being treated.

medication.title: Choose Medication
medication.desc: Select the medication from your farm's list. Withdrawal periods are shown automatically.

dosage.title: Dosage & Cost
dosage.desc: Enter the dosage given and the treatment cost for record-keeping.

healthLink.title: Link to Health Issue
healthLink.desc: Optionally link this treatment to an active health issue for the same cow.

save.title: Save Treatment
save.desc: Tap Save. If the medication has a withdrawal period, the cow will be flagged automatically.

stats.title: Breeding Overview
stats.desc: See pregnant, open, and due-soon counts at a glance.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.desc: Check for upcoming heats, pregnancy checks, calvings, and dry-off dates.

events.title: Recent Events
events.desc: View all breeding events — inseminations, pregnancy checks, calvings, and more.

logEvent.title: Log an Event
logEvent.desc: Tap + to record a new breeding event for any cow.

categories.title: Analytics Categories
categories.desc: Choose from Financial, Fertility, Health, or Structure analytics to explore your herd data.

kpis.title: Today's Snapshot
kpis.desc: These cards show today's key metrics — litres milked, active issues, and breeding actions due.

users.title: User Management
users.desc: Add workers, set PINs, and control what each user can access.

types.title: Custom Types
types.desc: Manage breed types, issue types, and medications to match your farm's needs.

featureFlags.title: Feature Flags
featureFlags.desc: Enable or disable features like breeding, milk recording, and analytics.

reports.title: Reports
reports.desc: Generate and download PDF or Excel reports for any date range.

auditLog.title: Audit Log
auditLog.desc: View a complete history of all changes made in the system.

list.title: User List
list.desc: Each card shows a user's name, role, status, and permission count.

badges.title: Role & Status
badges.desc: Badges show if a user is an admin or worker, and whether their account is active.

add.title: Add a User
add.desc: Tap + to create a new user. Choose admin (password) or worker (4-digit PIN) and set their permissions.

search.title: Search Medications
search.desc: Type a name to quickly find a specific medication.

list.title: Medication Cards
list.desc: Each card shows the medication name, active ingredient, and withdrawal periods.

withdrawal.title: Withdrawal Periods
withdrawal.desc: Coloured pills show milk and meat withdrawal times. Cows on these medications are automatically flagged.

add.title: Add Medication
add.desc: Tap + to register a new medication with its withdrawal periods and default dosage.

search.title: Search Issue Types
search.desc: Type a name to filter the list of health issue types.

list.title: Issue Type Cards
list.desc: Each card shows the emoji, name, code, and whether teat selection is required.

add.title: Add Issue Type
add.desc: Tap + to create a new health issue type with an emoji, name, and teat selection option.

list.title: Breed Types
list.desc: Each card shows a breed and its key reproductive timings.

timing.title: Timing Defaults
timing.desc: These values control auto-calculated breeding dates — gestation, heat cycle, pregnancy check, and dry-off days.

add.title: Add Breed Type
add.desc: Tap + to register a new breed with its reproductive timing defaults.

filters.title: Filter by Type
filters.desc: Tap a chip to show only changes to a specific entity — users, cows, treatments, etc.

advanced.title: Advanced Filters
advanced.desc: Narrow results by action type, user, or date range for detailed auditing.

entries.title: Audit Entries
entries.desc: Each entry shows what changed, who did it, and when. Tap 'Show Details' to see the old and new values.

types.title: Choose a Report
types.desc: Select the type of report you want — treatments, milk production, breeding, health, and more.

dates.title: Date Range
dates.desc: Set the start and end dates for the data you want to include in the report.

format.title: Output Format
format.desc: Choose PDF for a printable document or Excel for a spreadsheet you can analyse further.

generate.title: Generate Report
generate.desc: Tap Generate to create and download your report. It may take a moment for large date ranges.

globalDefaults.title: Defaults
globalDefaults.pushDescription: Push default data to existing farms. Only items that don't already exist on the farm will be added.
globalDefaults.pushConfirm: Push {type} defaults to {count} farm(s)? Only new items will be added — existing items will not be overwritten.
globalDefaults.noItems: No items yet. Add one using the + button.
globalDefaults.deactivateConfirm: Deactivate "{name}"? It will no longer be used when seeding new farms.
globalDefaults.deleteConfirm: Permanently delete "{name}"? This cannot be undone.

announcements.title: Announcements
announcements.deactivateConfirm: Deactivate this announcement? Users will no longer see it.
announcements.deleteConfirm: Permanently delete this announcement? This cannot be undone.

systemHealth.title: System Health
systemHealth.loadError: Could not load system health data.
